name: River straight on board 98765
description: The board itself is a 9-high straight (9-8-7-6-5). Any hand with a Ten or higher makes a higher straight; everything else chops. Tests the extreme case of 'board plays' river scenarios. Ranges use rank-based notation only — the parser doesn't accept specific-combo notation. The ranges are kept narrow (premium broadway + a few connectors) to make the chop / higher-straight split easy to analyze.

input.hero_range: TT, JJ, QQ, KK, AA, AQs, AKs, KQs, AJs, ATs, QJs, QTs, JTs, AJo, AQo, AKo, KQo, KJo, KTo, QJo, QTo, JTo
input.villain_range: TT, JJ, QQ, KK, AA, AQs, AKs, KQs, AJs, ATs, QJs, QTs, JTs, AJo, AQo, AKo, KQo, KJo, KTo, QJo, QTo, JTo

expected_notes: Populated by differential run. Degenerate river: hands with a Ten or higher make a higher straight; other hands chop with the board. Symmetric ranges (both sides have the same hand types) make this a good test of non-random-tiebreak behavior in the CFR solver — if hero and villain converge to materially different strategies here it's likely a bug.
